title: useQueryParam: URL state with type safety
scenario_context: Filter UI persists in URL — share links work. Naive: string parsing scattered. Need: typed param hook.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Back button doesn't restore filter.
reasoning: useSyncExternalStore subscribes to popstate. Replace vs push for transient vs durable.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tear-free via useSyncExternalStore. 2) Schema-validated. 3) Replace option.

title: useScript: dynamic script loader with status
scenario_context: Load third-party script (Stripe, Google Maps) on demand. Need single instance, status.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Stripe loaded twice — duplicate handlers.
reasoning: Map<url, Promise>. First call appends tag, subsequent share. Status tracked.
edge_cases_addressed: Script loaded outside hook — detect via tag query
test_strategy: Mock document.head; assert single script per src
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Cache across consumers. 2) Status state machine. 3) Error path.

title: useCopyToClipboard with feedback state
scenario_context: Copy button. User needs feedback ('Copied!' for 2s).
what_breaks_naive_approach: No feedback — user re-clicks unsure.
reasoning: navigator.clipboard.writeText with try/catch. Set state, reset via timer.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Cleanup on unmount. 2) Boolean return. 3) Reset timer.

title: useInfiniteScroll integrating useIntersectionObserver
scenario_context: Long list loads more on scroll-bottom. Naive scroll listener inefficient.
verification_chain: Layer 6: page tokens for cursor pagination
what_breaks_naive_approach: Triggers 10 fetches mid-scroll.
reasoning: IntersectionObserver on sentinel. Loading flag prevents double-fire. Cursor-based API.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Per page; show retry at bottom

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Sentinel + IntersectionObserver — efficient. 2) Loading guard. 3) Cursor not offset.

title: Circuit breaker for external API calls
scenario_context: Calling third-party service. When it's down, our app waits each call → cascading failure. Circuit breaker opens, fast-fails, recovers.
verification_chain: Layer 3: circuit breaker per external service
Layer 3: open → closed via test request
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No timeout — single slow call blocks
what_breaks_naive_approach: External service down; our requests pile up; thread pool exhausted; cascading failure.
reasoning: 3-state machine: CLOSED (calls pass), OPEN (immediate fail), HALF_OPEN (single test call). Threshold N failures in window → OPEN. Wait → HALF_OPEN. Success → CLOSED.
frontend_solution: // shows degraded notice when circuit open
edge_cases_addressed: Concurrent calls during half-open — one succeeds, others closed
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Bounded blast radius. 2) Auto-recovery. 3) Per-service scoped.

title: Graceful shutdown: drain connections before exit
scenario_context: Pod scaling/deploy kills containers. Naive: requests in-flight cut. Need: stop accepting new, finish in-flight, exit.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User mid-purchase gets reset connection — duplicate charge attempt.
reasoning: SIGTERM → stop accepting via server.close() (Node waits for active). Set Connection: close on responses. Force-exit timeout (e.g., 30s).
frontend_solution: // retries safe with idempotency
test_strategy: Send SIGTERM during request; in-flight completes; new rejected
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Drain pattern. 2) Force-timeout for hangs. 3) Connection:close hint.

title: Banner stack management: severity ordering and dismiss persistence
scenario_context: Multiple banners possible (past_due, quota, deprecation). Limit visible, prioritize, persist dismiss.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User dismisses past_due banner; reload re-shows.
reasoning: Banner registry with priority. Display top-1. Dismiss → store in localStorage with banner ID + version (so urgent updates re-trigger).
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) One at a time — uncluttered. 2) Versioned dismiss — urgent updates reach. 3) Priority ordering.

title: Versioned API responses with backward compat
scenario_context: v1 and v2 API coexist. Schema differences. Need clear separation.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Old clients see new fields and break
what_breaks_naive_approach: Adding required field to v1 — breaks integrations.
reasoning: Separate /v1 and /v2 routers with their own schemas. Shared services; schemas transform between versions. v1 frozen for breaking changes.
backend_solution: // server/routes/v1.ts and v2.ts; transformers per route
frontend_solution: // SDK exports v1 and v2 — apps pin version
edge_cases_addressed: Field added to v2, missing in v1
test_strategy: Hit v1 and v2; assert distinct shapes
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Hard version split. 2) Transformers at boundary. 3) Telemetry-driven sunset.

title: Tenant-aware logging: every log line includes tenant
scenario_context: Debugging cross-tenant — logs without tenant identifier useless. Auto-inject from context.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Logs without tenant for tenant ops
what_breaks_naive_approach: Bug investigation needs tenant context — must reconstruct from request IDs.
reasoning: AsyncLocalStorage holds child logger with tenantId. Anywhere in request scope, getLogger() returns it. Logs automatically include.
frontend_solution: // N/A
edge_cases_addressed: Error in middleware before tenant set
test_strategy: Assert every log entry has tenantId
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Context-bound. 2) Auto-propagate. 3) Audit coverage.

title: Tax calculation per region (Stripe Tax)
scenario_context: Charging EU + US customers — different VAT/sales tax. Stripe Tax handles. Naive: skip tax → compliance nightmare.
verification_chain: Layer 3: subscription with automatic_tax: { enabled: true }
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No tax — illegal in many regions
No VAT ID field for EU B2B
what_breaks_naive_approach: EU customer hit with VAT they didn't see — chargeback.
reasoning: Stripe Tax computes automatic. Customer's address is the input. VAT ID for B2B reverse-charge. Display tax in checkout preview.
frontend_solution: // client uses Stripe Checkout; tax visible there
edge_cases_addressed: Address change after purchase — adjustments via tax credit
Multi-region same customer — based on billing address
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Use Stripe Tax — compliance-as-a-service. 2) VAT ID field. 3) Tax shown pre-purchase.

title: Empty state CTA paths: import, template, or start fresh
scenario_context: New users overwhelmed by blank. Offer 3 paths: import existing data, choose template, start fresh.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Empty state with single CTA — limits
No import — users start from scratch
what_breaks_naive_approach: Blank dashboard with 'New Project' button — user bounces.
reasoning: Three cards with icon + description. Track which converts.
edge_cases_addressed: Template suggestion based on signup answer
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Multiple paths. 2) Track conversion. 3) Each path teaches.

title: API key with scoped permissions
scenario_context: User creates API key for integration. Default = full access. Better: scopes (read-only, write-only, specific resources).
verification_chain: Layer 4: middleware validates scope per endpoint
what_breaks_naive_approach: Key with full access leaks → all data exposed.
reasoning: Generate key, store hash + scope list. On request, check route's required scope against key scopes. Last-used timestamp.
frontend_solution: // client/components/ApiKeyForm.tsx — scope checkboxes, copy key once

error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Create new key with broader scope

edge_cases_addressed: Key with no scope → can't do anything
test_strategy: Key with scope 'read'; GET works, POST 403
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Scoped > all-or-nothing. 2) Hashed storage. 3) Last-used for rotation.

title: Recharts stacked bar with totals on top
scenario_context: Bar shows breakdown by category. Want total atop each bar.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User sums mentally.
reasoning: Recharts LabelList computes sum per stack, positions above.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Use Recharts primitive. 2) Sum precomputed. 3) Last bar shows total.

title: Visx histogram with d3-array bin
scenario_context: Distribution of values (response times, ages). Need bin selection (Sturges/Scott).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Bad bin choice hides pattern.
reasoning: d3-array bin().thresholds() supports auto. Scott/Sturges built in.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) d3-array bin. 2) Auto thresholds. 3) Visx for rendering.

title: Chart.js doughnut with dynamic plugins per dataset
scenario_context: Multiple datasets with per-dataset behaviors (different tooltips, callbacks).
what_breaks_naive_approach: All tooltips look the same.
reasoning: datalabels plugin per dataset. tooltip callbacks with payload context.
frontend_solution: // client/components/MultiDoughnut.tsx — config per dataset
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Per-dataset config. 2) Tooltip callbacks. 3) Plugin scope.

title: ECharts dataset transform for client-side pivot
scenario_context: Server returns long-format data. Chart needs wide-format. Use ECharts dataset transforms.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Lodash pivot on 10k rows is slow.
reasoning: ECharts dataset transforms (filter, sort, ecStat) run in C++ (ECharts native). Faster + declarative.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Native transforms. 2) Declarative. 3) Performant on large data.

title: Tremor with skeleton loading matching card dimensions
scenario_context: Loading cards without CLS. Tremor cards have specific heights — skeleton must match.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Cards jump 200px when load — frustrating.
reasoning: Skeleton component mirrors Card + Metric + AreaChart with placeholder blocks at correct sizes.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-028 MetricCardSkeleton
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Match dimensions exactly. 2) Pulse animation respects reduced motion. 3) No CLS.

title: Print-friendly chart styles
scenario_context: Charts in PDF reports. Default colors waste ink, animation lost.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Color charts → grayscale loses series distinction.
reasoning: @media print swaps to high-contrast palette + pattern fills. Hide tooltip/legend interactive bits.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Print is first-class. 2) Pattern fills not just color. 3) Hide interactives.

title: Two-phase resource provisioning: reserve then commit
scenario_context: Creating user with workspace + DB schema + billing account is multi-step. Partial failure leaves orphans.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User created, billing fails, ghost user.
reasoning: Two-phase: reserve (record intent), commit (per step idempotent), finalize. On failure, rollback each completed step.
frontend_solution: // retry-safe via idempotency
test_strategy: Inject failure at each step; assert rollback
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Track state for rollback. 2) Per-step idempotency. 3) Alert on rollback fail.

title: What if rate limit hit during retry — exponential backoff with jitter
scenario_context: Client retries on failure but hits rate limit during retry burst. Backoff with jitter prevents thundering herd.
what_breaks_naive_approach: All clients retry at same time on recovery → server pile-up.
reasoning: backoff = min(maxMs, baseMs * 2^attempt) * (0.5 + random*0.5). Cap at maxRetries. Respect Retry-After header.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-001
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Jitter prevents herd. 2) Retry-After respected. 3) Bounded attempts.

title: Server emits TypeScript declarations for shared types
scenario_context: Server is monorepo with shared types. Build emits .d.ts to be consumed by client.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Manual type duplication drifts.
reasoning: npm workspaces or pnpm. Shared package exports types. Compile order ensured.
frontend_solution: // imports @repo/shared
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Monorepo for shared types. 2) Build order. 3) Single source.

title: Tenant-level webhooks: tenant can subscribe to events
scenario_context: Tenants want to receive their events at their endpoint. Need: subscription store, secure signing, retry.
verification_chain: Layer 3: event publisher loops tenant subscriptions
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tenant's endpoint slow → our API slow. Async + retry needed.
reasoning: On event, enqueue per subscription. Worker delivers with HMAC sig. Retries 5x exponential. Dead-letter for human triage.
frontend_solution: // client/components/WebhookSettings.tsx — manage subscriptions, view delivery log
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Async — no impact on our API. 2) HMAC for security. 3) Dead-letter for failures.

title: Stripe checkout success + cancel flows with state recovery
scenario_context: User goes to Stripe Checkout. They might complete, cancel, or close tab. App must handle each.
verification_chain: Layer 3: checkout session created with success_url + cancel_url
Layer 8: success route polls until webhook completes
Layer 8: cancel route restores plan picker
what_breaks_naive_approach: User cancels checkout — app still shows 'pending payment'.
reasoning: Stripe redirects to success/cancel URLs. Success page polls subscription endpoint until status changes. Timeout after 30s with explainer.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Don't trust URL — wait for webhook. 2) Polling with timeout. 3) Cancel UX.

title: Workspace slug generation with collision avoidance
scenario_context: Workspace name 'Acme Corp' → slug 'acme-corp'. If taken, append suffix. Reserved words blocked.
verification_chain: Layer 4: slug-availability endpoint for live check
what_breaks_naive_approach: Two simultaneous signups grab 'acme'.
reasoning: Generate base slug, check, append -2, -3... if taken. Block reserved (admin, api, auth, etc.). Unique index as final guard.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Reserved list. 2) Live availability check. 3) Unique index for race.

title: Cross-tenant invitation for partner orgs
scenario_context: Agency works with multiple client tenants. User belongs to multiple workspaces. Switcher UI.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Agency user creates separate account per client.
reasoning: Memberships table. JWT includes available tenants. Active tenant in session/cookie. Switcher dropdown.
edge_cases_addressed: Active tab vs new tab — cookie scope
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Memberships > one-to-one. 2) Per-switch new token. 3) Cookie scoping.

title: Recharts composed chart: line + bar + reference area
scenario_context: Sales chart: bars for daily sales, line for moving avg, reference area for sale promotion period.
verification_chain: Layer 8: multiple Y-axes if scales differ
what_breaks_naive_approach: Promotion bump invisible without annotation.
reasoning: ComposedChart accepts Bar, Line, Area children. ReferenceArea highlights x-range.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Reference area for context. 2) ComposedChart for mix. 3) Moving avg precomputed.

title: Visx network graph with link strength visualization
scenario_context: Org connections with weighted edges. Thicker line = stronger connection.
what_breaks_naive_approach: All edges same — no signal of strength.
reasoning: Link strokeWidth scales with weight. Hover dims unrelated. Click drills into node neighborhood.
frontend_solution: // extend e2e-018 OrgGraph with edge.weight
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Visual encoding of weight. 2) Hover for focus. 3) Force layout.

title: Chart.js bubble chart with sized + colored bubbles
scenario_context: 3D data (x, y, size). Bubble size = magnitude.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Linear radius misleads — area should encode value.
reasoning: Radius = sqrt(value / Math.PI) so AREA is linear to value. Sort by radius desc so smaller drawn on top.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Area scaling not radius. 2) Sort for overlap. 3) 3D encoding (x, y, area).

title: ECharts calendar heatmap for daily activity
scenario_context: GitHub-style activity calendar. Year of daily values colored.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Outlier day dominates color scale.
reasoning: echarts calendar layout. Use min/max from non-outlier range (p95). Mark today.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) p95-based scale resists outliers. 2) Calendar coordinate system. 3) Locale-aware week start.

title: Long category labels: rotate, truncate, or wrap
scenario_context: Bar chart with category names like 'Q4 Marketing Campaign'. Default ticks overlap.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Full text inline pushing chart down
what_breaks_naive_approach: Labels overlap; unreadable.
reasoning: Truncate to N chars + ellipsis. Angle 45deg if many. Tooltip shows full.
frontend_solution: // truncate logic\nfunction truncateTick(value: string, maxLen = 12) { return value.length > maxLen ? value.slice(0, maxLen) + '…' : value; }
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Truncate + ellipsis + tooltip. 2) Angle if needed. 3) Mobile-friendly.
